diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis due to progranulin gene mutation (CLN11)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of progressive retinopathy, recurrent generalized seizures, moderate ataxia, and subtle cognitive dysfunction in two siblings. Key evidence included retinal dystrophy, polyphasic visual-evoked potential waveform suggesting hyperexcitability of the occipital cortex, selective cerebellar atrophy on MRI, and fingerprint storage material in skin biopsy with absence of progranulin protein.
